name: Jardin Anglais (Geneva lakefront)
description: Despite being in Geneva's UHI core, lake breeze and mature trees create strong comfort island

name: Parc des Bastions (Geneva)
description: Walled park traps cool air under dense canopy, feels 5-8°C cooler than adjacent streets

name: Jet d'Eau viewing area
description: Zero shade but water spray and wind create dramatic cooling perception

name: Bains des Pâquis
description: Public baths: psychological and physical cooling despite being in UHI zone

name: Quai du Mont-Blanc promenade
description: Comfort comes from view and breeze, not shade

name: Place de la Navigation (Lausanne-Ouchy)
description: Historic lakefront square, mature trees plus lake = high comfort

name: Parc de Milan (Lausanne)
description: Urban park in steep terrain, cold air pools here

name: Esplanade de la Cathédrale (Lausanne)
description: Exposed but the view of blue lake creates psychological cooling

name: Place de la Palud (Lausanne)
description: Narrow medieval square, buildings provide all-day shade, fountain adds sensory cooling

name: Quai de la Rouvenaz (Montreux)
description: The Montreux promenade: lake + palms + Chillon view = peak comfort perception

name: Château de Chillon surrounds
description: Uniquely multi-sensory: stone, water, history, mountain views

name: Cully wine harbour
description: Small harbour: water proximity, building shade, intimate scale = high comfort

name: Morges lakefront / Parc de l'Indépendance
description: Celebrated park, tulip season adds color and life perception

name: Nyon lakefront / Château terrace
description: Elevated position above lake gives both view and breeze

name: Vevey marketplace / Grande Place
description: Large open square, comfort varies dramatically between market days and empty

name: Pont du Mont-Blanc (Geneva)
description: Bridge over water but feels hostile: traffic, exhaust, no stopping places

name: Route de Meyrin corridor
description: 4-lane arterial: asphalt radiation + noise + no green = extreme perceived heat

name: Parking Les Halles (Lausanne)
description: Surface parking in city center: dark surfaces radiate, no psychological relief

name: Gare de Lausanne forecourt
description: Large exposed concrete area, crowds add body heat, acoustic stress

name: Prilly-Malley commercial zone
description: Archetypal Swiss commercial zone: 100% sealed, zero comfort design

name: IKEA/Crissier retail strip
description: Extreme thermal discomfort: dark surfaces, HVAC waste heat, no green

name: Route de Genève (Lausanne west)
description: Major arterial through Ouest lausannois, continuous heat corridor

name: Place du Tunnel (Lausanne)
description: Dark urban canyon, trapped air, noise amplification = perceived worse than measured

name: Vernier-Meyrin junction area
description: Highway interchange: pure infrastructure zone, uninhabitable thermal environment

name: Avenue de France (Geneva)
description: International quarter, wide avenue but insufficient canopy for canyon effect

name: Place de l'Europe (Lausanne-Flon)
description: Elevated platform over Flon valley, concrete radiates, no natural cooling

name: Montreux rail station area
description: Transit hub with sealed surroundings, despite mountain air nearby

name: Flon district (Lausanne)
description: Comfort flips: cool morning shade in narrow streets, hot noon sun on plazas, evening social warmth

name: Lavaux wine terraces (general)
description: Summer: vine canopy creates dappled shade, stone walls radiate at night; winter: exposed and cold

name: Vidy lakefront (Lausanne)
description: Exposed to bise wind (cold NE): comfortable in summer, hostile in winter

name: Rue de Bourg (Lausanne)
description: Historic shopping street: shade from height-to-width ratio, stone absorbs heat but looks cool

name: Grand-Rue Vevey
description: Perception shifts with activity: market mornings feel alive-cool, empty afternoons feel trapped-hot

name: Avenue de la Gare (Montreux)
description: Mountain view creates psychological cooling despite standard urban conditions
